steps.title: Identify the function of the underlined sentence
steps.content: The sentence opens with "However" — a contrast signal — and reports that new studies show horizontal gene transfer (HGT) is more common in eukaryotes than originally thought. The prior sentence established the accepted view: HGT "has rarely been observed among eukaryotes." The underlined sentence directly challenges that established belief.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It argues that a particular direction of research concerning horizontal gene transfer is likely to be fruitless.': The sentence encourages reconsideration, not pessimism about research directions. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It indicates a distinction between horizontal gene transfer and vertical gene transfer.': That distinction is made in the first sentence of the passage, not in the underlined sentence. ✗
  • Choice C — 'It implies that a common perception of horizontal gene transfer may be inaccurate.': The sentence contradicts the previously held view that HGT rarely occurs in eukaryotes — exactly what this choice describes. ✓
  • Choice D — 'It compares the frequencies with which horizontal gene transfer has been detected in two categories of organisms.': The sentence contrasts a new finding with an older belief, not a direct frequency comparison between prokaryotes and eukaryotes. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
